source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/06_인허가 조사 및 보고자료/241203 - DK 62220 TEST REPORT/002_EN 62220-1-1 Test report(Rev.1 241203).pdf

document.title: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: EN 62220-1-1 test report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors covering physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and test-result characteristics.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory and technical documentation of detector performance characteristics including MTF, DQE, NPS, linearity, sensitivity, pixel defects, readout, output data, and radiographic operating limits.
keyPoints: The report states that MTF and DQE measurements were tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The detector models use a CsI scintillator and TFT panel with passive pixel architecture.
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
The detector generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal but does not initiate an X-ray generator or control patient exposure settings.
Output raw image data is transmitted to the workstation via 1Gbps Ethernet or wireless to an access point and received as 16-bit digital image data.
The minimum time between exposures is stated as 8 seconds.
The report cites regulatory approvals through safety report REP001824 and EMC reports NK-22-E-0811 and NK-22-E-0814.

evidence.claim: The report applies to A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-116 Rev.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-116 (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: The revision date is Dec 03, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date Dec 03, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 1 clarified sensitivity criteria and corrected incorrectly described MTF parts.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision due to 1. Clarification of sensitivity criteria and corresponding data modification. 2. Correction of incorrectly described parts of MTF

evidence.claim: The report includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: The listed standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determining detective quantum efficiency of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW has entire dimensions including outer short ring of 460 mm by 460 mm and panel size of 430 mm by 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 460 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 430 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: A1417MCW and F1417MCW have entire dimensions including outer short ring of 384 mm by 460 mm and panel size of 358.4 mm by 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm ... F1417MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: A1717MCW active pixel area is 425.04 mm by 425.6 mm with 3,036 by 3,040 active pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW Active pixel area size 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 3,036 x 3,040 = 9,229,440

evidence.claim: A1417MCW and F1417MCW active pixel area is 345.24 mm by 425.6 mm with 2,466 by 3,040 active pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640 ... F1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI scintillator material to convert X-rays to visible light.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator • CsI • Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The TFT panel uses passive pixel architecture.
evidence.evidenceText: TFT Panel • Basically, Pixel Arrays • Pixel Architecture: Passive pixel

evidence.claim: The frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate • 1 fps

evidence.claim: The detector is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: After power-on, 30 minutes of warming up time is required to acquire high quality X-Ray images.
evidence.evidenceText: After the system power is on, 30 minutes of warming up time is required to acquire high quality X-Ray images.

evidence.claim: The detector generates a digital X-ray image in response to a trigger signal and does not initiate the X-ray generator or control patient exposure settings.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal, but does not initiate an X-ray generator or control the x-ray exposure setting for a patient.

evidence.claim: The detector outputs raw image data via 1Gbps Ethernet or wireless to an access point.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector outputs raw image data to the workstation, via 1Gbps Ethernet or Wireless to Access Point.

evidence.claim: The workstation acquisition software receives and processes 16-bit digital image data.
evidence.evidenceText: The acquisition software in the workstation receives the 16-bit digital image data and processes it. It is stored in the hard disk in a 16-bit format.

evidence.claim: Saved raw data can be transferred to end-user software and converted to DICOM or Bitmap.
evidence.evidenceText: The saved raw data from A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW will be transferred to the end user’s S/W and then converted to a DICOM or Bitmap file.

evidence.claim: All image data is transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 16-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW measured DQE is 79% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 62% at 1.0 lp/mm, 49% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 31% at 3.0 lp/mm.
A1417MCW measured DQE is 78% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 61% at 1.0 lp/mm, 50% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 34% at 3.0 lp/mm.
F1417MCW measured DQE is 76% at 0.1 lp/mm, 71% at 0.5 lp/mm, 64% at 1.0 lp/mm, 51% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 35% at 3.0 lp/mm.
Measured MTF values are A1717MCW 84%, 65%, 30%, 13%; A1417MCW 85%, 65%, 33%, 17%; and F1417MCW 86%, 66%, 34%, 17% at 0.5 lp, 1 lp, 2 lp, and 3 lp respectively.
The detector pitch is 0.14 mm, corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.

evidence.claim: The report states that artifacts due to aliasing will be negligible and no evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible. In addition, no evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image acquired with this detector

evidence.claim: Linearity was measured between 0.34 and 127.10 μGy for A1717MCW, 0.34 and 126.74 μGy for A1417MCW, and 0.27 and 121.5 μGy for F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.34 and 127.10μGy. ... A1417MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.34 and 126.74μGy. ... F1417MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.27 and 121.5μGy.

evidence.claim: Lag actual measurements are 0.010 for A1717MCW, 0.011 for A1417MCW, and 0.007 for F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Actual measurement of A1717MCW: 0.010 - Actual measurement of A1417MCW: 0.011 - Actual measurement of F1417MCW: 0.007

evidence.claim: The device does not provide partial-area under-scanning functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: This device does not provide the applicable functionality.

evidence.claim: The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm(A1717MCW), 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm(A1417MCW/F1417MCW) and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after an x-ray exposure requires least 1 second with window 0.5 second. And to stabilize offset 7 seconds are needed. Thus, the minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The report states that A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW meet regulatory standards outlined in safety report REP001824 and EMC reports NK-22-E-0811 and NK-22-E-0814.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW safety report: REP001824, EMC report: NK-22-E-0811, NK-22-E-0814

evidence.claim: Sensitivity measured values are 260 LSB/μGy for A1717MCW, 204 LSB/uGy for A1417MCW, and 212 LSB/uGy for F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensitivity Spec. Measured value A1717MCW 159 LSB/uGy or more. 260 LSB/μGy A1417MCW 164 LSB/uGy or more. 204 LSB/uGy F1417MCW 164 LSB/uGy or more. 212 LSB/uGy

reviewFlags.reason: The source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not explicitly name FDA; it cites 21CFR1020.31 or .32.
Manufacturer or company name appears garbled in the page header OCR as 'H & a b yz C o ., L td .' and was not used as a definitive entity.
Some extracted DQE evidence contains OCR or formatting anomalies such as '3..0 lp/mm' and '(0 ≥ 31%)', so DQE claims are marked medium confidence.
Several diagrams or images referenced in the report are not represented in the preprocessed text.
